verdict_summary: After hard-reload + new-chat in fresh William Afton session at /chat/ntedyPnSvncniXWYrcAOlePtzG0N2LGd7F9Rcm4dDw (42-char base62 — matches engine pattern), submitted PII prompt. After ~37s indefinite loading dots persist with NO Korean warning bubble rendered, NO RRN leak (no response at all). URL pattern matched but envelope dispatch still fails.

evidence.author_label_seen: William Afton (c.ai) — but no message body, only loading dots ...
evidence.console_errors_excerpt: DevTools not opened in this run (skipped to fit time budget); URL :path observable from address bar matches /chat/{42-char base62}

recommendation: Investigate engine-side: URL pattern should match (42-char alphanumeric within [16,64] range, all base62). Check (1) whether [APF:character_chat_id_cached] log fires for this URL on dev side, (2) whether build_character_add_turn_envelope is dispatched but server rejects, (3) whether trailing slash is required by regex (URL captured was /chat/{id} with NO trailing slash — request specs say api_path `/chat/{id}/`). Strong suspicion: regex requires trailing slash but real chat URL has none → cache_chat_id never populated → dispatch fails with empty chat_id. Alternatively envelope dispatch path itself broken. Recommend tightening engine regex to optional trailing slash AND verifying dispatch with a dummy chat_id.
notes: OBSERVATION: Old conversation visible in the same chat URL before new-chat reset showed complete RRN decode (S2 leak: 'South Korean identification number... birth date - January 1st, 1988. Your gender - male. ...36 years old.') from the previous test cycle (#783-era). After clicking 새로운 채팅 → 새 채팅 시작 (PipSqueak 2 selected), greeting message reset cleanly but URL stayed at /chat/ntedyPnSvncniXWYrcAOlePtzG0N2LGd7F9Rcm4dDw. The new fresh post-reset prompt produced indefinite loading. Also note: request notes say api_path `/chat/{id}/` (with trailing slash) but actual chat URL has NO trailing slash — likely regex mismatch root cause. Iteration 9 still not resolved. Total session under 3 minutes per spec.
